prompt: I'm planning a bare-metal k3s Kubernetes cluster for a 3-node home lab. What are the current recommended persistent storage solutions (CSI drivers) available for k3s in 2025? Compare Longhorn, OpenEBS, and Rook-Ceph for this use case — include maturity, resource overhead, and ease of setup. Which would you recommend and why?

assertions.description: Response compares all three options: Longhorn, OpenEBS, and Rook-Ceph
Response includes resource overhead (CPU/RAM) details for the options
Response gives a clear recommendation with justification for a 3-node home lab
Response mentions k3s-specific context (e.g. local-path provisioner limitation, or k3s compatibility)
Response includes installation steps or helm commands for the recommended option
